intitule: Lesson 2 Assessment: Official Procedures - Administrative Vocabulary & Polite Requests

enonce.texte:
Read the definitions and complete the sentences below using the correct administrative vocabulary from the box. Then, transform the statements into polite questions using the modal verbs can, could, or may.

Vocabulary Box: application form, birth certificate, fee, submit, passport, receipt, deadline, officer

questions.texte: Match each definition to the correct word.
questions.reponse: 1. The official document proving a person's identity and nationality for travel: Passport. 2. The money you must pay for a service: Fee. 3. A document you fill out to request something officially: Application form. 4. The last day you can do something: Deadline. 5. To hand in a document for review: Submit.

sousQuestions.texte: The official document proving a person's identity and nationality for travel: _______
The money you must pay for a service: _______
A document you fill out to request something officially: _______
The last day you can do something: _______
To hand in a document for review: _______

questions.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct word from the box. Then, rewrite the sentence as a polite question.
questions.reponse: See each sub-question for specific answers.

sousQuestions.texte:
Statement: You need to get a/an _______ to prove where you were born.
Polite Question: _______
sousQuestions.reponse: birth certificate. Could you please tell me where I can obtain a birth certificate?

sousQuestions.texte:
Statement: The _______ for this passport application is 15,000 FCFA.
Polite Question: _______
sousQuestions.reponse: fee. May I know if the fee for the passport application is 15,000 FCFA?

sousQuestions.texte:
Statement: You must _______ your documents before 4 PM.
Polite Question: _______
sousQuestions.reponse: submit. Can you tell me where I should submit my documents before 4 PM?

intitule: Lesson 2 Assessment: Listening & Form Filling (Real-Life Competency)

enonce.texte:
You are at the 'Douala Central Passport Office'. Listen to the audio recording of an official giving instructions to first-time applicants. (If no audio is available, read the transcript below). Based on the information provided, fill out the attached sample passport application form completely and correctly.

Transcript (Official Recording):
"Good morning, all. For a first-time passport application, please follow these steps carefully. First, you must obtain a birth certificate from your local council. Then, collect a green application form from counter number 3. Fill the form using black ink only. Do not use correction fluid. You will need to attach two recent passport photos, 4x4 cm, with a white background. The processing fee is 75,000 FCFA for a standard 32-page passport. Pay this at counter 5. After payment, submit your complete file at counter 7 before 2 PM on any weekday. The passport will be ready for collection exactly 15 working days later. Keep your payment receipt; it is your proof."
images: A blank passport application form with the following fields: Surname, First Name, Date of Birth (DD/MM/YYYY), Place of Birth, Nationality, Profession, Permanent Address, Phone Number, Type of Application (First-time/Renewal), Number of Pages (32/48), Signature of Applicant, Date of Submission, Official Stamp Area.

questions.texte: Analyze the instructions from the recording and accurately complete the application form below.
questions.reponse: The completed form must contain specific information from the transcript: Black ink only, 2 photos (4x4cm, white background), fee of 75,000 FCFA for 32 pages, submit before 2 PM weekdays at counter 7, collection after 15 working days, keep receipt.

sousQuestions.texte: According to the instructions, what type of ink must you use to fill the form?
Calculate: If you submit your application on Monday, October 14th, 2025, what exact date will your passport be ready for collection? (Assume no public holidays).
sousQuestions.reponse: Monday, November 4th, 2025. (15 working days = 3 full weeks, excluding weekends).

sousQuestions.texte: Critique the following statement: 'You can pay the fee and submit the file at the same counter.' Justify your answer using the recording.
sousQuestions.reponse: The statement is false. According to the recording, you must pay the fee at counter 5, and then submit your complete file at counter 7. These are different counters.

intitule: Lesson 2 Assessment: Writing a Formal Email of Request (Competency Task)

enonce.texte:
You are a student in Yaoundé. You need to obtain a certified copy of your birth certificate from the 'Mfoundi District Council' to register for the national exam. However, you live far away and cannot go in person. Write a formal email to the Chief of the Civil Registry.

In your email, you must:

  • Introduce yourself politely.
  • Explain the situation (need for certified copy, distance).
  • Politely ask about the procedure (using can, could, may).
  • Request the required documents and the total fee.
  • Ask if a representative can submit the documents for you.

questions.texte: Write a well-structured formal email (200-250 words) following the instructions above.

sousQuestions.texte: Evaluate the following email subject line: 'I need papers'. Propose a more appropriate subject line.
sousQuestions.reponse: The subject line 'I need papers' is too informal and vague. A better subject line would be: 'Request for Information: Obtaining a Certified Copy of Birth Certificate - Student, Yaoundé'.

sousQuestions.texte: Construct two polite questions from the list below using could and may. (1) ask about fee. (2) ask about using a representative.
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. 'Could you please inform me of the total fee for obtaining a certified copy of my birth certificate?' 2. 'May I send a representative to submit the documents on my behalf since I cannot travel?'
